question: Based on Table 1.3-1 (37 assemblies) and Table 5.3-2 (beltline chemistry), is the reduced core size beneficial for RPV integrity?

nuscale_ch05_0013.title: References
nuscale_ch05_0013.summary: This section lists references and standards used in the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report regarding the integrity of the reactor coolant boundary. It includes guidelines and reports from the Electric Power Research Institute and the American Society of Mechanical Engineers, as well as tables summarizing relevant ASME code cases for reactor components.

nuscale_ch01_0558.title: DSRS 12.3-12.4, Rev 0: Radiation Protection Design FeaturesII.9 RG 8.15
nuscale_ch01_0558.summary: This section summarizes NuScale’s conformance with various regulatory guides related to radiation protection design features, as outlined in DSRS 12.3-12.4. It details whether each guide is applicable, not applicable, conforms, or partially conforms, with references to supporting sections or tables for further information. Specifically, RG 8.15 is noted as not applicable to the NuScale design.

nuscale_ch01_0052.title: Comparison with Other Facilities
nuscale_ch01_0052.summary: This section compares the major design features and parameters of the NuScale Power Plant with those of a typical pressurized water reactor (PWR). It highlights differences in size, output, and system components, showing that NuScale uses significantly smaller modules, fewer fuel assemblies, and simplified systems compared to conventional PWRs. The comparison is summarized in tables for easy reference.

nuscale_ch05_0015.title: Reactor Vessel Materials
nuscale_ch05_0015.summary: The Reactor Vessel section describes the NuScale Power Module's reactor pressure vessel (RPV), which houses the reactor core, steam generators, and pressurizer within a single vessel, serving as a key barrier to fission product release. The RPV and its components are constructed from materials specified by the ASME Boiler and Pressure Vessel Code to ensure integrity and safety throughout the plant's design lifetime.

nuscale_ch05_0017.title: Reactor Vessel Integrity
nuscale_ch05_0017.summary: This section outlines the procedures and regulatory compliance measures to ensure the integrity of the NuScale reactor vessel throughout its 60-year design life. It details the development of operating procedures to maintain pressure-temperature limits, evaluates the vessel’s resistance to pressurized thermal shock and upper-shelf energy degradation, and confirms that predicted material properties remain within regulatory safety criteria under anticipated operating conditions.

nuscale_ch05_0016.title: Pressure-Temperature Limits, Pressurized Thermal Shock, and Charpy Upper-Shelf Energy Data and Analyses
nuscale_ch05_0016.summary: This section outlines the basis for establishing operational pressure and temperature limits for the reactor coolant pressure boundary (RCPB) to comply with regulatory requirements over the plant’s 60-year lifespan. It describes the use of ASME and NRC methodologies to calculate pressure-temperature limit curves, accounting for vessel embrittlement, and provides the relevant figures and tables that define safe operating conditions to prevent non-ductile failure of the reactor vessel.

nuscale_ch05_0018.title: References
nuscale_ch05_0018.summary: This section outlines the inspection, shipment, installation, and inservice surveillance requirements for the NuScale reactor vessel, referencing relevant ASME and ASTM standards. It specifies procedures for maintaining a dry, controlled environment during shipment and details the applicable codes and standards used for quality assurance and material testing.

nuscale_ch01_0376.title: SRP 9.4.2, Rev 3: Spent Fuel Pool Area Ventilation SystemII.2 Compliance with GDC 5
nuscale_ch01_0376.summary: The NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report states that the Spent Fuel Pool Area Ventilation System complies with General Design Criterion (GDC) 5, as outlined in SRP 9.4.2, Rev 3. There are no exceptions or additional comments noted for this compliance, indicating full conformance with the regulatory requirement.

nuscale_ch01_0768.title: DSRS 15.5.1-15.5.2, Rev 0: Chemical and Volume Control System Malfunction That Increases Reactor Coolant InventoryII.4.C Input Parameters and Initial Conditions - Core Burnup
nuscale_ch01_0768.summary: This section summarizes NuScale’s conformance with regulatory criteria for the Chemical and Volume Control System malfunction that increases reactor coolant inventory, specifically regarding input parameters and initial conditions such as core burnup. It states that NuScale’s design conforms to the relevant DSRS sections, and clarifies that certain SRP guidance related to pressurizer relief valves is only partially applicable, as NuScale uses spring-loaded safety valves rather than power-operated relief valves. The section also references conformance tables for further details.

nuscale_ch01_0064.title: NuScale Test and Inspection Plans
nuscale_ch01_0064.summary: This section describes NuScale’s test and inspection plans for key reactor components, including confirmation of control rod drive shaft operability and emergency core cooling system (ECCS) valve performance. The ECCS valve test program demonstrated that the valves, including unique features like the inadvertent actuation block (IAB), function properly under reactor operating conditions, with testing conducted at a specialized facility to simulate real-world pressures and temperatures.

nuscale_ch05_0020.title: Steam Generators
nuscale_ch05_0020.summary: The steam generators (SGs) in the NuScale Power Module are integrated within the reactor pressure vessel (RPV), which also houses other key components such as the pressurizer, safety valves, and emergency cooling systems. The SG system includes feedwater and main steam piping, thermal relief valves, and access ports, with the RPV itself forming the outer pressure boundary and the SG tubes contributing to the reactor coolant pressure boundary.

nuscale_ch05_0006.title: System Evaluation
nuscale_ch05_0006.summary: This section describes the evaluation of the NuScale Power Plant’s system performance, focusing on optimizing steady-state parameters such as coolant temperatures, flow rates, and pressures to maximize electrical generation and maintain safety margins. Calculations using thermal hydraulic software and hand verification account for uncertainties and ensure the natural circulation design is effective, with low pressure losses and robust performance across design conditions.

nuscale_ch05_0021.title: Reactor Coolant System Piping
nuscale_ch05_0021.summary: The section discusses the design and degradation assessment of the NuScale reactor coolant system's steam generator (SG) tubing. It notes that the main degradation observed in similar operating reactors is wear from flow-induced vibration or foreign objects, and that NuScale's design—with its helical tubing and novel support structure—does not increase risk compared to existing designs. Prototypic testing and standard SG program guidelines are deemed sufficient, with wear identified as the most likely degradation mechanism and corrosion risks assessed as low, supporting a 40% through-wall defect plugging criterion.

nuscale_ch05_0014.title: Reactor Vessel
nuscale_ch05_0014.summary: The Reactor Vessel section describes the NuScale Power Module (NPM), which integrates the reactor core, steam generators, and pressurizer within a single reactor pressure vessel (RPV) surrounded by a containment vessel. The RPV serves as a key pressure-retaining component, housing critical reactor systems and providing structural support, while its materials and fabrication comply with stringent ASME Boiler and Pressure Vessel Code requirements to ensure long-term integrity and safety.

edges.description: Section A lists references supporting the technical basis for reactor coolant boundary integrity, while Section B outlines regulatory criteria and conformance related to radiation protection design features, connecting them through the need to demonstrate that design and operational choices (such as reduced core size and beltline chemistry) satisfy regulatory requirements for RPV integrity.

Section B provides the core size data (Table 1.3-1) necessary to assess RPV integrity, while Section A lists references that may include methodologies or standards for evaluating RPV integrity, thus Section A supports the analysis of data from Section B.

Section A provides references to industry standards and guidelines that inform the evaluation of reactor vessel materials and chemistry discussed in Section B, which describes the RPV and its materials in the context of the NuScale design.

Section B (Table 1.3-1) provides the reduced core size (37 assemblies) design value, which is a parameter that Section A (DSRS 12.3-12.4) uses to evaluate conformance with radiation protection and RPV integrity regulatory criteria.

Section A outlines regulatory criteria and conformance for radiation protection design features, while Section B describes the physical design and materials of the reactor pressure vessel (RPV), with both sections connected through the requirement that the RPV design and materials (Section B) must satisfy the radiation protection and integrity criteria specified in Section A.

Section A outlines regulatory criteria and conformance related to radiation protection design features, which provide the framework and requirements that Section B's discussion of reactor vessel integrity and operating procedures must satisfy to ensure RPV integrity under the reduced core size scenario.
